title: Battle of Beta Celestine
content:
May 22th, 2429

 

Equestrian, Human, Sarden Ships Present

  • ESAV Neon Lights: Manehattan-Class Patrol Ship - Destroyed
  • USS Shanghai: China-Class Cruiser - Destroyed
  • USS Bandundu: Congo-Class Corvette - Destroyed

Total: 3 Ships: 3 Destroyed, 0 Survived

 

Huachavi-Apleen Alliance Ships Present

  • MDS Mandran II: D-18 Destroyer - Destroyed
  • MDS Macraque II: C-22 Corvette - Survived
  • MDS Macraque III: C-22 Corvette - Destroyed
  • MDS Macraque IV: C-22 Corvette - Survived
  • MDS Mamosey I: C-22 Corvette - Survived

Total: 5 Ships: 2 Destroyed, 3 Survived

To prevent the complete destruction of the civilian ships fleeing the surprise attack on Yildun Rest, the decision was made that the three ships not carrying civilians would turn around and face the Apleen vanguard force led by the MDS Mandran II. The fleets met in the uninhabited system of Beta Celestine, the survivors catching the Apleen off-guard, not expecting such a bold (and suicidal) maneuver from them. Despite having the element of surprise, the already wounded survivors were unable to do much damage against the fleet; and were only able to conventionally destroy the MDS Macraque III.

 

The USS Bandundu was the second ship to be lost, its bridge destroyed by a full salvo from the Mandran II, followed shortly by the USS Shanghai. Completely outmatched as the weakest ship in the engagement, the captain of the ESAV Neon Lights gave the order to ram the Mandran II. Punching a hole through its shields at the final moment, the Neon Lights impacted the Mandran II directly in the bridge section, and dealing the final, fatal blow of the battle. Due to the loss of its command ship, the Apleen vanguard was forced to halt its pursuit, and thanks to the sacrifice of the Neon Lights, Shanghai, and Bandundu, the survivors of Yildun Rest managed to get home safely.
